meaning: adj.牛顿的,牛顿学说的n.信仰牛顿学说的人
examples: Voltaire's book commanded a wide audience, according to Feingold, because "he was neither a mathematician nor a physicist, but a literary giant aloof from the academic disputes over Newtonian ideas".
